message:
test(speedscore): add edge case tests for normalization and weight handling

Expands test coverage for SpeedScore calculation edge cases that could
cause unexpected behavior in production scoring.

New test cases:
- test_normalize_equal_refs_returns_midpoint: When low and high reference
  values are equal, normalize() should return 50.0 (midpoint) rather than
  dividing by zero or returning NaN

- test_speedscore_weights_zero_sum_fallback: When all weights are zero
  (invalid configuration), normalize() should fall back to default weights
  rather than producing NaN scores

- test_speedscore_single_component_matches_cpu_score: When only CPU
  benchmark results are available, the total SpeedScore should equal
  the CPU component score (verifies weight distribution logic)

These tests ensure robust handling of degenerate inputs that could occur
from misconfiguration or partial benchmark data, preventing silent scoring
failures that would affect worker selection accuracy.

Part of SpeedScore reliability improvements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
